sections.title: 1. Strengthening the fight against digital fraud
path: ANDEMA's Proposals for the Digital Fairness Act (DFA)
1. Strengthening the fight against digital fraud

sections.title: 2. Custody and destruction of online counterfeits

sections.title: 3. Strengthening criminal law and cross-border enforcement
path: 3. Strengthening criminal law and cross-border enforcement

paragraphs.text: The Spanish Association for the Defense of Trademarks (Andema) welcomes the opportunity to provide feedback on this consultation and supports the proposal to exempt counterfeit products from the ban on destruction, as such products not only infringe the rights of brands but also fail to comply with EU chemicals and consumer safety legislation, leading to serious and significant risks for consumer health, safety and the environment. These counterfeit goods contaminate EU textile waste streams with residues of restricted substances, undermining the efforts undertaken by Member State authorities as well as economic operators to achieve circularity. It should be recognized that the existence of counterfeit products on the EU market is the root cause of their negative environmental impact, which would be exacerbated if they were introduced to consumers and ultimately into EU waste streams following re-use, recycling or donation. The only way to prevent this negative environmental impact and potential threat to consumer health and safety is to establish measures that stop counterfeits from entering the EU and effectively deter infringers. Furthermore, we recommend clarifying that economic operators whose rights have been infringed should not be responsible for the destruction and subsequent reporting of counterfeit products, as this should instead be handled by the competent authorities that prevented their circulation on the EU market. Finally, we believe that the requirement to retain documentation for a period of 10 years would result in a disproportionate administrative burden, as it exceeds the retention periods for business records in some Member States, and we therefore propose limiting the retention period to five years to ensure proportionality while still enabling authorities to review destruction practices where necessary.

section_path: ANDEMA's Proposals for the Digital Fairness Act (DFA)
1. Strengthening the fight against digital fraud
paragraphs.text: Fully integrate the notification, rapid removal, and seller traceability (KYBC) obligations already provided for in the DSA.

section_path: ANDEMA's Proposals for the Digital Fairness Act (DFA)
1. Strengthening the fight against digital fraud
paragraphs.text: ANDEMA wishes to support and promote the efforts of the Spanish National Commission on Markets and Competition (CNMC), which has been designated in Spain as the coordinator for the implementation of the Digital Services Act (DSA), actively collaborating in the development of this regulatory framework. We consider this mechanism particularly useful for addressing issues such as counterfeiting, impersonation, and fraudulent reviews, as it ensures traceability and legal certainty in the removal of illegal content.

section_path: ANDEMA's Proposals for the Digital Fairness Act (DFA)
1. Strengthening the fight against digital fraud
paragraphs.text: The activation of this system is essential to ensure effective protection against online infringements and to provide legal certainty for all stakeholders involved in the digital ecosystem. Activating this system is essential to ensure effective protection against online infringements and to provide legal certainty for all stakeholders involved in the digital ecosystem.

section_path: ANDEMA's Proposals for the Digital Fairness Act (DFA)
1. Strengthening the fight against digital fraud
paragraphs.text: Incorporate brand protection clauses in digital public procurement to prevent the purchase of counterfeit or non-compliant products.

section_path: 2. Custody and destruction of online counterfeits
paragraphs.text: Establish a harmonised EU protocol for the swift removal and destruction of counterfeit products seized in the digital environment.

section_path: 3. Strengthening criminal law and cross-border enforcement
paragraphs.text: Include in the DFA a framework for agile cross-border criminal cooperation in counterfeiting and online fraud cases.

section_path: 3. Strengthening criminal law and cross-border enforcement
paragraphs.text: Request the creation of a specialised industrial property prosecutor's office with clear powers over online counterfeiting offences, following the model of Circular 1/2006.

section_path: 4. Prevention of brand vulgarisation
paragraphs.text: Emphasise that the proliferation of counterfeiting and misuse in the digital sphere accelerates the vulgarisation of trademarks.

section_path: 4. Prevention of brand vulgarisation
paragraphs.text: Include consumer education measures to help distinguish authentic from counterfeit products and reinforce the value of legitimate brands.

section_path: 5. Cybersecurity and domains
paragraphs.text: Ensure that the DFA enables the swift blocking at EU level of fraudulent domain registrations linked to counterfeit activities.

section_path: 6. Opposition to generic packaging
paragraphs.text: Recall that restrictions on the legitimate use of trademarks (e.g. plain packaging) weaken brand protection and encourage illicit trade.

section_path: 6. Opposition to generic packaging
paragraphs.text: Promote the visibility and traceability of authentic brands rather than limiting them.

section_path: 6. Opposition to generic packaging
paragraphs.text: An de de Ma Asociación para la defensa de la Marca

section_path: 7. Promotion of brand sponsorship
paragraphs.text: Consider incentives for companies, particularly SMEs, to invest in digital social responsibility and consumer education on responsible purchasing.

section_path: 8. Coordination and communication
paragraphs.text: Establish clear guidelines, FAQs, and application manuals to ensure consistent implementation of the DFA by companies, consumers, and authorities.

section_path: 8. Coordination and communication
paragraphs.text: Propose the creation of a European working group with the participation of brand associations to ensure correct and harmonised implementation.
